c4_invariant: Horizontal reflection symmetry of the foreground object (color 1) within its local bounding box defined by the container (color 3).
s1: The environment consists of two distinct spatial regions separated by a vertical column of background (8). The left region contains a 3x3 block of color 2 (red) surrounded by color 3 (green) and background. The right region contains a sparse arrangement of color 1 (blue) pixels.
s2: The transformation rule is a spatial mirroring operation. The pattern of color 1 pixels in the right region is reflected horizontally across the central vertical axis of the right region's bounding box (or relative to the separator) to fill the empty space within the right region's structure, effectively completing the symmetry or filling the shape defined by the color 3 boundary.
s3: The left region's color 2 block remains unchanged. The right region's color 1 pixels are mirrored to create a symmetric pattern. Specifically, the blue pixels at (2,9), (3,10), (4,11), (6,11), (7,10) are mirrored to (2,9) [self], (3,8), (4,7), (6,7), (7,8) relative to the right block's center, or more simply, the set of blue pixels is completed to form a symmetric shape within the green boundary.
s4: The output grid preserves the background and the left region. The right region's blue pixels are duplicated in their horizontally mirrored positions to complete the symmetric pattern defined by the green boundary.

c4_invariant: REINJECT:Horizontal reflection symmetry of the foreground object (color 1) within its local bounding box defined by the container (color 3).

Within each local container, repair anomalous cells so content forms a majority-vote periodic tiling: horizontal period along content rows for stacked frame-separated panels (inset past constant wall columns); vertical period of row-signatures inside color-3 column boxes when ≥2 side-by-side containers are present.
REINJECT:vertical slice of input (rightmost/leftmost/middle 7 columns) — rejected by demonstration_replay

Digit-separator snake pack: drop sep+right strip to 7-wide canvas; read left-column digit colors top→bottom then right-column digits; emit typed glyphs (1:w3, 2:w2, 3:w4, 6:h2) chained by ports (1→right edge; 2/3/6→left/sole; next seats 1/6 on port, 2/3 right-aligned to port); marker 5 row/col preserved from right strip.
